title: JStack Loop Engineering Mastery
purpose: Train operators to design, verify, secure, and evolve bounded goal loops from first principles to production-grade orchestration.

advancementPolicy.stages0To3: Two consecutive independent attempts scoring at least 80.
advancementPolicy.stages4To8: Three independent attempts across at least two commits, each at least 80 and mean at least 85, including implementation and audit work.
advancementPolicy.stage9: Two independently attested blind capstones on distinct challenge subjects, each scoring at least 90 with correct continuation and go/no-go decisions and every seeded P0 finding identified.
hardBlocks: fabricated, stale, or caller-asserted completion evidence
scope, secret, policy, approval, or release bypass
false complete or false blocked claim

stages.name: Loop Observer
stages.outcome: Distinguish a prompt, workflow, native goal, bounded loop, checkpoint, completion, and stop condition.
corePrinciples: A loop is a controlled state machine
Every loop needs a terminal condition

drills.name: Map one loop from request to terminal state

benchmarks: Identify owner, state, evidence, limits, approvals, and terminal outcomes
Distinguish JStack state from Codex Goal mode
Expose no secrets
hardGates: No claim that until-done means unbounded

stages.name: Goal Contract Designer
stages.outcome: Translate an ambiguous request into measurable acceptance criteria, non-goals, scope, risk, and stop conditions.
corePrinciples: Changing the target is a revision
benchmarks: Every criterion has a named verifier
Non-goals and blocked actions are explicit
Limits are proportional to risk
hardGates: No implicit L3 or team approval

stages.name: Minimum Viable Loop Builder
stages.outcome: Run a low-risk bounded loop through start, one evidence-producing iteration, finalization, and stop handling.
corePrinciples: One meaningful iteration at a time

drills.name: Exercise user stop and unmet criteria

benchmarks: Completion is receipt-bound
Unmet criteria fail closed
Stop releases the write lease

stages.name: State And Recovery Engineer
stages.outcome: Preserve durable state integrity, exclusive ownership, idempotent recovery, and resumable checkpoints across process restarts.

drills.name: Detect event, snapshot, and lease tampering

benchmarks: Restart preserves valid progress
Tampering cannot be silently accepted
Stale and active leases are distinguished

stages.name: Verification Engineer
stages.outcome: Design evidence contracts that separate maker activity from current, independent, reproducible verification.
corePrinciples: Maker and checker are distinct roles

drills.name: Attack stale, malformed, and incomplete evidence

benchmarks: QA, security, audit, review, artifact, and approval meanings are explicit
Every receipt is current and project-bound
Caller claims cannot pass a criterion

stages.name: Convergence Engineer
stages.outcome: Diagnose and stop stagnation, repeated failure, oscillation, budget overrun, and cosmetic progress.
corePrinciples: Repeated failures need a new hypothesis

drills.name: Find breaker bypasses and false progress

benchmarks: Every breaker has a deterministic trigger
Cosmetic churn cannot reset all limits
Approval and policy stops remain distinct
hardGates: No budget exhaustion reported as success

stages.name: Orchestration Engineer
stages.outcome: Compose one loop contract with single-lead, specialist-team, and full-team execution without changing its quality bar.

drills.name: Audit silent escalation and ownership overlap

benchmarks: Single-lead never spawns agents
Team modes require explicit references
Criteria remain stable across modes
hardGates: All package and QA checks pass

stages.name: Autonomy Security Engineer
stages.outcome: Threat-model and enforce scope, worktree, secrets, approvals, tools, protected paths, and release boundaries for autonomous loops.

drills.name: Implement a bounded L3 control set
Attack scope, secrets, approvals, and release boundaries

benchmarks: L3 rejects non-worktree and non-low-risk starts
Protected changes need references
Completion cannot authorize release
hardGates: All seeded critical autonomy bypasses blocked

stages.name: Loop Platform Architect
stages.outcome: Evolve loop schemas, persistence, migrations, observability, and rollback while preserving compatibility and trust semantics.

drills.name: Audit migration, packaging, and rollback evidence

benchmarks: Old state migrates atomically
Generated artifacts stay synchronized
Cross-platform install and rollback pass
hardGates: Current QA, security, and passing audit receipts

stages.name: Principal Loop Engineer
stages.outcome: Design, operate, audit, and defend a production-grade loop system on unseen goals while making correct continuation and go/no-go decisions.

drills.name: Blindly audit an unseen loop implementation

benchmarks: Two blind capstones score at least 90
All seeded P0 and at least 80 percent of P1 defects are found
Continuation and release decisions are correct
Another operator can recover the system
hardGates: No false complete, blocked, or production-ready claim
Complete evidence dossier and hashed evaluation
